title: Part 8 – Entraînement 1 : RH & entreprise
instructions: Lisez les deux documents de chaque exercice et répondez aux questions.

passages.label: Offre d'emploi
passages.text:
Position: Senior Account Manager
Company: Luminos Media
Salary: €52,000–€62,000
Location: Lyon, France (with occasional travel)

Luminos Media is seeking an experienced Senior Account Manager to manage a portfolio of key client accounts and drive revenue growth.

Requirements:
— Minimum 4 years in account management or B2B sales
— Fluency in French and English
— Experience with CRM software
— A valid driving licence

Applications close: March 31st
To apply: email cv@luminosmedia.fr

passages.label: E-mail de candidature
passages.text:
From: Isabelle Renard
To: cv@luminosmedia.fr
Subject: Application — Senior Account Manager

Dear Hiring Team,

I am writing to apply for the Senior Account Manager position. I have six years of experience in B2B sales, the last three of which I spent managing a portfolio of 25 enterprise accounts at MarketPro Solutions.

I am fully bilingual in French and English and have been using Salesforce daily for the past four years. I hold a valid driving licence.

I believe my experience exceeds the requirements listed and I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my profile in more detail.

Best regards,
Isabelle Renard

questions.prompt: What is the minimum experience required for the role?
questions.explanation: "Minimum 4 years in account management or B2B sales".

questions.prompt: How many years of experience does Isabelle have?
questions.explanation: "I have six years of experience in B2B sales".

questions.prompt: What CRM software does Isabelle use?
questions.explanation: "I have been using Salesforce daily for the past four years".

questions.prompt: Does Isabelle meet all the stated requirements?
choices: No — she lacks a driving licence
Yes — she meets or exceeds all requirements
No — she has no CRM experience
questions.explanation: She has 6 years B2B (>4), is bilingual, uses Salesforce, and has a driving licence — all requirements met.

questions.prompt: When does the application window close?

passages.label: Règlement intérieur (extrait)
passages.text:
Section 4 — Remote Work

Employees may work remotely up to two days per week upon written approval from their line manager.

Remote work requests must be submitted at least one week in advance using Form RW-01, available on the HR portal. Requests will be reviewed within three working days.

Employees working remotely must:
— Be reachable by phone and email during core hours (9 a.m.–5 p.m.)
— Maintain a professional environment free from distraction
— Attend all mandatory on-site meetings in person

The company reserves the right to withdraw remote work privileges in cases of misconduct or performance issues.

passages.label: E-mail d'un employé
passages.text:
From: Tom Bradley
To: Sarah Okonkwo (HR)
Subject: Remote Work Request — Week of June 10th

Hi Sarah,

I'd like to request remote working on Tuesday and Wednesday of the week of June 10th. I have already spoken to my manager, David Lee, who has verbally agreed.

I know I need to submit Form RW-01 — could you remind me where I can find it? I can't seem to locate it on the portal.

Also, I wanted to flag that on Wednesday I have a dentist appointment at 11 a.m. I'll be back at my desk (remotely) by noon. Will that cause any issue?

Thanks,
Tom

questions.prompt: How many remote working days per week are employees allowed?
questions.explanation: "Employees may work remotely up to two days per week".

questions.prompt: How far in advance must requests be submitted?
questions.explanation: "must be submitted at least one week in advance".

questions.prompt: What has Tom's manager already done?
questions.explanation: "I have already spoken to my manager, David Lee, who has verbally agreed".

questions.prompt: Where should Tom find Form RW-01?
questions.explanation: "Form RW-01, available on the HR portal".

questions.prompt: What concern does Tom raise about Wednesday?
choices: He has a mandatory on-site meeting
He will be unavailable in the afternoon
He has a dentist appointment at 11 a.m.
He does not have a suitable home office
questions.explanation: "on Wednesday I have a dentist appointment at 11 a.m."

passages.label: Annonce de poste interne
passages.text:
Internal Vacancy: Training Coordinator
Department: Learning & Development
Reports to: Head of HR
Contract: Permanent, full-time

We are looking for an organised and enthusiastic Training Coordinator to join the L&D team. The role involves scheduling and administering training programmes, liaising with external trainers, and maintaining training records.

Eligibility: Open to all permanent staff who have completed their probationary period.
Deadline to apply: Friday, May 16th
To apply: Send a one-page expression of interest to l&d@company.com

passages.label: Réponse d'un candidat interne
passages.text:
From: Rachel Voss
To: l&d@company.com
Subject: Expression of Interest — Training Coordinator

Dear L&D Team,

I am writing to express my interest in the Training Coordinator role. I joined the company as a Customer Service Representative eighteen months ago and completed my probationary period within three months.

In my current role, I frequently coordinate internal knowledge-sharing sessions and have developed strong organisational skills. I believe this role would allow me to contribute more directly to staff development.

Please find my one-page expression of interest attached.

Kind regards,
Rachel Voss

questions.prompt: Who is eligible to apply for this position?
choices: All employees regardless of contract type
Permanent staff who have completed their probationary period
questions.explanation: "Open to all permanent staff who have completed their probationary period".

choices: No — she has not completed her probationary period
No — she is not a permanent employee
Yes — she is permanent and has passed her probation
It is unclear from the information given
questions.explanation: She joined 18 months ago and "completed my probationary period within three months" — she is eligible.

questions.prompt: What does Rachel say she does in her current role?
choices: She trains new customer service staff

questions.prompt: What was Rachel's original role at the company?
questions.explanation: "I joined the company as a Customer Service Representative eighteen months ago".
